intro: Filgueirasia cannavieira is a plant species native to South America, classified as a member of the family Sapindaceae. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including gastrointestinal issues, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Filgueirasia cannavieira include triterpenoids, flavonoids, and alkaloids, which are believed to be responsible for its therapeutic effects. In traditional herbal medicine, Filgueirasia cannavieira is commonly prepared as an infusion or tincture, and is often used to treat digestive problems, such as diarrhea and dyspepsia.
